subject: Cloud Computing
title: Mainframes: The First Shared Computers
description: A fast breakdown of Mainframes: The First Shared Computers for builders.

hook.text: In the 1960s, one computer filled a room. Hundreds of people shared it through terminals.

buildup.text: IBM's mainframes dominated business. Companies rented them because buying one cost millions.

discovery.text: Users connected via dumb terminals — screens with no processing power of their own.

twist.text: This sounds ancient, but it's the same model as today's cloud: many users, one powerful machine.

climax.text: Mainframes never died. Banks and airlines still run critical systems on them right now.

punchline.text: The cloud isn't new. It's mainframes with better marketing.

quiz.question: How does the mainframe model relate to modern cloud computing?
options: Both share a powerful central machine among many users
Mainframes and cloud have nothing in common
Mainframes were personal computers for individual use
